decisions.summary: Session name changes from per-bead (inspect-<issue>-<beadSlug>) to per-issue (inspect-<issueLower>). Per-issue is the persistent identity across all bead inspections.

Inspector uses print-mode (-p flag via launcher promptFile) not interactive mode. Claude exits after each bead; remain-on-exit on keeps the tmux pane open. Resume is via --resume <savedClaudeSessionId> -p continuationPrompt on the next bead — no deliverAgentMessage needed.

Continuation prompt = short bead brief only (bead description + diff scope + verdict signaling). Full Jidoka/constraint-compliance instructions survive in JSONL history. No full-prompt re-issue on resume.
Context budget = never hard-reset. Claude's built-in compaction handles context growth over a long issue. No inspectionCount threshold.
Model guard: if resolveModel() returns a different model than the sessionModel stored in runtime.json, force a cold spawn (new randomUUID(), full prompt). Same-model = warm resume.
BLOCKED retry uses warm resume — the inspector session that issued the BLOCKED verdict retains its reasoning, improving re-inspection accuracy.

Deacon crash watchdog must check isPaneDead in addition to sessionExists. remain-on-exit on keeps sessions alive after exit, so sessionExists alone can no longer distinguish a running inspector from a finished one.

hazards.summary: Implementation checkpoint: does 'claude --resume <uuid> "$prompt"' correctly deliver the continuation prompt as the next user turn? The launcher generator supports resumeSessionId + promptFile together (verified at launcher-generator.ts:531-554), but empirical verification in a real session is required.
hazards.mitigation: Work agent should test: run claude --resume <some-uuid> 'test message' against an existing session and confirm the message is received as a new turn. If not, fallback: launch with --resume only (no -p) then deliver via deliverAgentMessage tmux path.

hazards.summary: The per-issue agentDir (inspect-<issueLower>/) is shared across all bead inspections. launcher.sh and task-prompt.md are overwritten per bead. This is safe because each bead is sequential, but if two beads were somehow dispatched concurrently, the files would race.
hazards.mitigation: Concurrent dispatch cannot happen in normal pipeline operation (work agent waits for verdict before proceeding). Document this assumption as a non-goal for concurrency safety.

hazards.summary: The killSession call at the start of spawnInspectAgentPromise will kill the remain-on-exit dead pane, then create a fresh tmux session. This is correct — but if killSession fails silently and createSession fails because the session name is taken, the spawn will fail.
hazards.mitigation: The existing .catch(() => {}) on killSession is intentional (session may not exist). createSession already errors on duplicate names; the Error will propagate and return { success: false }. The work agent handles this and retries.

hazards.summary: getAgentRuntimeStateSync reads from the in-process mirror (empty in fresh CLI processes). When pan inspect is invoked from the CLI (not the dashboard server), the mirror is empty and the function returns null — forcing a cold spawn even when runtime.json has saved state.
hazards.mitigation: Check if getAgentRuntimeStateSync is reliable in CLI context. If not, use getAgentRuntimeStateSync's underlying file-read path (patchRuntimeJson reads from disk). Alternatively: read runtime.json directly with readFileSync as a fallback. The work agent should verify this during implementation.

sessionHistory.note: Planning session started for PAN-1951: Inspector agent should resume a warm session instead of cold-spawning a new one per bead

vBRIEF written. 6 beads: per-issue-session-naming, inspect-remain-on-exit, inspect-continuation-prompt, inspect-state-persistence (all simple/parallel), inspect-resume-dispatch (complex, requiresInspection), deacon-inspect-liveness (simple). Key decisions: print-mode + remain-on-exit + --resume approach; short continuation prompt; no context budget reset; model guard on sessionModel mismatch.
